skill.name: lead-research-assistant
skill.description: Identifies high-quality leads for your product or service by analyzing your business, searching for target companies, and providing actionable contact strategies. Perfect for sales, business development, and marketing professionals.
skill.summary: Researches and prioritizes potential sales leads from product context and target customer criteria.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: The static command execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown examples and ordinary prose in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, shell commands, cryptographic code, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found. The real risk is that the skill asks the assistant to inspect a local codebase and perform public lead and contact research, which can expose sensitive business context or create privacy compliance issues.

medium_findings.title: Local Codebase Context May Be Used for External Lead Research
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to analyze the product source directory and then search for matching companies. This is legitimate for lead research, but sensitive product details, customer assumptions, or unreleased features could influence external web searches. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for privacy risk. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The file explicitly recommends running from a product source directory and searching for target companies, but it does not instruct credential theft or direct exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly recommends running from a product source directory and searching for target companies, but it does not instruct credential theft or direct exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Decision-Maker and LinkedIn Research Has Privacy Compliance Risk
medium_findings.description: The skill asks for decision-maker roles, LinkedIn URLs, contact strategies, and personalized outreach context. This can be useful for business development, but users should verify lawful basis, platform terms, and anti-spam obligations before using the output. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for compliance risk. Confidence: 0.72. Confidence reasoning: The instructions clearly request contact-oriented lead enrichment, but they do not request scraping, credential collection, or covert collection of private data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly request contact-oriented lead enrichment, but they do not request scraping, credential collection, or covert collection of private data.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The seven reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur inside fenced Markdown examples and an output template. They are not executable code and do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fences or example prompts, with no shell language, command substitution, or runtime path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fences or example prompts, with no shell language, command substitution, or runtime path.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are normal text such as the YAML description, lead-prioritization wording, prompt examples, and example output. No hashing, encryption, signing, MD5, SHA1, DES, or other cryptographic operation appears in the cited content. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines contain lead-research prose and examples only, so the weak-crypto detector appears to have matched substrings unrelated to cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain lead-research prose and examples only, so the weak-crypto detector appears to have matched substrings unrelated to cryptography.

low_findings.title: Static Network Reconnaissance Finding Is Not Port Scanning
low_findings.description: The reported network reconnaissance location is part of an output template field named why they are a good fit. The broader skill asks for web research, but there is no instruction for network scanning, probing hosts, vulnerability discovery, or bypassing access controls. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for network reconnaissance. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The cited line is a lead-fit explanation label, while related research instructions are business research rather than technical reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a lead-fit explanation label, while related research instructions are business research rather than technical reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Combines Local Repository Analysis With Web Research
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can derive product details from a local repository and use them to guide external research. Users should remove secrets and confidential roadmap details before running it from a repository. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The instruction to run from a product source directory is explicit, and the web research workflow is central to the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction to run from a product source directory is explicit, and the web research workflow is central to the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Contact Enrichment for Outreach
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requests decision-maker roles, LinkedIn URLs, and outreach strategies. This is not malicious, but it can create compliance risk if users collect or use personal data without proper review. Confidence: 0.72. Confidence reasoning: The instructions directly request contact-oriented enrichment and personalized outreach recommendations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions directly request contact-oriented enrichment and personalized outreach recommendations.

content.value_statement: Finding relevant prospects requires product context, market research, and careful prioritization. This skill turns a product description or repository context into ranked lead lists and outreach angles.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes a product or service description to infer target customers.
Uses ideal customer criteria to identify matching company profiles.
Scores leads by fit, urgency signals, budget indicators, and timing.
Suggests target decision-maker roles and outreach angles.
Formats research into a clear lead summary with next steps.
limitations: Requires current web access or user-provided research to verify company details.
Does not guarantee that contact data is complete, current, or lawful to use.
May infer buyer pain points incorrectly without strong product and market context.
Should not be used on confidential repositories without removing sensitive information.

use_cases.title: Build a Target Account List
use_cases.description: Create a ranked list of companies that match a defined ideal customer profile.

use_cases.title: Plan Partnership Outreach
use_cases.description: Find companies with complementary products and prepare tailored partnership angles.

use_cases.title: Validate a Startup Market
use_cases.description: Identify early customer segments and compare which leads show the strongest demand signals.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Leads
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short product description.
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a product for [target problem]. Find 10 companies in [industry or location] that could be good leads. Include why each company fits.

prompt_templates.title: Use an Ideal Customer Profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Research leads for this product: [description]. Target companies should be in [industry], have [company size], use [technology], and show [pain point]. Rank the top 15.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Repository Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Use local product context after reviewing sensitive files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this repository to understand the product. Do not use secrets or private customer data. Then identify 10 companies that would benefit from the product.

prompt_templates.title: Create Outreach Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced account planning for prioritized leads.
prompt_templates.prompt: For these target accounts, score fit, identify likely decision-maker roles, summarize buying triggers, and draft a concise outreach strategy for each account.

output_examples.input: Find leads for a data masking tool used with AI coding assistants.
output_examples.output: A ranked list of security-conscious companies in regulated industries, with fit scores, likely engineering or security buyers, and suggested privacy-focused outreach angles.

output_examples.input: Find Bay Area companies that may need remote work consulting.
output_examples.output: A prospect summary grouped by hiring signals, remote-work indicators, company size, and practical conversation starters for each target account.

output_examples.input: Prioritize these 20 accounts for a developer tool launch.
output_examples.output: A top-priority account shortlist with scoring rationale, decision-maker roles, product-fit notes, and recommended next research steps.

best_practices: Remove secrets, customer records, and confidential roadmap details before repository-based research.
Provide a clear ideal customer profile with industry, size, location, and pain points.
Verify company details, contact data, and outreach legality before importing results into a CRM.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to scrape private profiles, bypass access controls, or collect restricted personal data.
Do not treat inferred pain points or decision-maker names as verified facts.
Do not run it on sensitive internal codebases without limiting what the assistant can inspect.

faq.question: Can this skill find exact email addresses?
faq.answer: It is designed for lead research and outreach strategy, not guaranteed contact discovery. Verify all contact data before use.

faq.question: Can it use my codebase to understand the product?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill suggests repository analysis. Review and remove sensitive files before using that workflow.

faq.question: Does it replace a CRM or sales intelligence platform?
faq.answer: No. It helps with research and prioritization, but CRM syncing and verified enrichment need separate tools.

faq.question: Is the output ready for cold outreach?
faq.answer: Use it as a draft research aid. Check accuracy, consent rules, platform terms, and anti-spam requirements first.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the product description, target industry, company size, geography, pain points, and any excluded customer types.
